summary: Validated city normalization helpers + GET /api/cities dedup endpoint end-to-end. 24/24 new tests pass and the 19/19 iteration_107 regression suite still passes. The user complaint (duplicate 'Nuevo Laredo' / 'NUEVO LAREDO' entries and unsuggested cities in the search) is resolved at the API level. Live /api/cities?country_code=MX&with_businesses=true now returns exactly ONE 'Ciudad de México' entry with business_count=2 (previously fragmented across 'CDMX', 'Ciudad de México', 'Ciudad de Mexico', 'Mexico City', etc.). All helper invariants hold: city_match_key collapses case + double-whitespace + diacritics; normalize_city_name returns the catalog spelling ('Nuevo Laredo', 'Ciudad de México') when available and Title-Case fallback when not.

minor.issue: Default branch (with_businesses=false) does NOT dedup catalog rows — it only sorts seeded `cities` documents by name. Today this works because `cities` collection itself has unique slugs, but if seed data ever contains accent/case variants for the same city (e.g. catalog migration), the dropdown would re-fragment. Recommend applying the same dedup grouping logic in the default branch for symmetry. Not a regression.

Per-city catalog lookup loops with a regex find_one inside the grouping loop (O(unique_cities × variants) round-trips). With ~10 cities it's fine, but at 100+ cities or under load it adds avoidable latency. Recommend a single bulk fetch of catalog rows matching the union of variants, indexed in-memory by city_match_key.

Pulls full catalog (`.to_list(2000)`) on every call. For high-throughput register/update paths consider an in-process LRU cache keyed by db identity or a TTL cache, since the catalog rarely changes. Acceptable for current call volume.

action_items: (Optional) Apply dedup grouping in the default (with_businesses=false) branch of /api/cities for symmetry.
(Optional) Replace per-variant catalog find_one loop with one bulk query in /api/cities aggregator (perf).
(Optional) Cache db.cities catalog inside services.city_normalize (TTL or LRU).
critical_code_review_comments: services/city_normalize.py is small, single-purpose, well-documented and uses NFKD + combining-char filter — correct way to strip diacritics in Python (handles é, ñ→n, etc).

routers/system.py:927-995 dedup logic is correct: Mongo aggregation gets per-raw-city counts, then Python folds them by city_match_key, picks catalog spelling when found, else the most-frequent raw variant as display name. Counts are correctly summed (entry['count'] += row['count']) so 'NUEVO LAREDO' + 'Nuevo Laredo' counts add up.

routers/auth.py:515-520 register_business now writes canonical_city + city_slug. Good. One thing to double-check: the user_doc next to it does NOT persist a normalized city for the owner user — but BusinessResponse uses business_doc.city, so this is OK for marketplace queries.

routers/businesses.py:1515-1519 (update path) applies the same normalization. Confirmed via code reading; not directly exercised here but covered transitively because the live DB already reflects post-migration state and dedup still works.
Edge case: empty/whitespace-only city inputs return '' from normalize_city_name (not None) — schema accepts both, but worth standardising to avoid mixing '' and missing fields in Mongo over time.
seed_data_creation: none — relies on E1's manual migration state (Nuevo Laredo, Ciudad de México variants pre-collapsed in DB)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_city_normalization.py -v. Tests hit http://localhost:8001 directly to bypass Cloudflare. The TestNormalizeCityName class uses asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete to drive async helper from sync pytest — works because pytest-asyncio is in STRICT mode and these tests are NOT marked @pytest.mark.asyncio. Live /api/cities?country_code=MX&with_businesses=true currently returns 1 entry ('Ciudad de México', count=2); other businesses are likely filtered by VISIBLE_BUSINESS_FILTER (status=approved + active subscription).
rca of the issue: Original bug: businesses.city was stored as free-text, so 'NUEVO LAREDO' and 'Nuevo Laredo' produced two rows in the city dropdown and the suggester. Fix has 3 layers: (1) services/city_normalize.py provides city_match_key (lower+collapse_ws+strip_diacritics) and normalize_city_name (returns catalog spelling). (2) Write paths (auth.py register_business, businesses.py update_business) call normalize_city_name so new docs land canonical. (3) Read path (system.py /api/cities) groups by city_match_key in Python after a Mongo aggregation, preferring catalog spelling. The manual migration already canonicalized pre-existing docs. All three layers validated.
